description: Illustrate the thing the narration is about as one held full scene; bring on-screen text on top per the script — baked into the scene if static from the start, or appearing (as a cut-out overlay) when the narration introduces it.
whenToUse: A captioned scene / stat reveal: draw the narrated subject (a place, group, object, diagram) and caption it (a number, a label, a term) when it is spoken. Repeat + hard-cut for a list of items.
generalize: The scene, the field, the text content, whether the text is BAKED or APPEARS, and the text voice are all SLOTS from the script. One instance (e.g. an isolated people + a handwritten population stat) is an example.
produces: gpt-image-2 SCENE (subject + environment, top/space left empty) as bgUrl; BAKED text is written into the scene prompt (surfaces locked); APPEARING text = its own gpt-image-2 asset (voice) via magenta color-key -> timed overlay hard-appearing at its startWord.

scene.note: SHORT plain description of the held scene (subject + environment + optional map/motif), with the caption area left empty; no style words

item.voice: a voice from text-styles (title-bold | label-handwritten | in-world)

mode.note: baked = present from frame 0 (written into the scene image); appears = cut-out overlay hard-appearing at startWord

item.startWord: for mode=appears: the narration word it lands on
item.yFrac: for mode=appears: center Y (default ~0.12 for a top caption)

styleLock: STYLE_BLOCK_IMAGE prepended to the scene; appearing text is gpt-image-2 lettering (never a font). Baked text: put the exact string in the scene prompt and lock all other surfaces ('no other text/letters').
rules: Baked vs appears is decided by the SCRIPT timing (§4a): spoken mid-beat -> appears at that word; present from the start -> baked.
Text is gpt-image-2 art in a chosen voice; never a compositor font.
For a list: repeat this recipe per item, hard-cut on a palette change (rotate field).
